title: Ensure that the RotateKubeletServerCertificate argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the RotateKubeletServerCertificate argument is set to true
input_row.description: Enable kubelet server certificate rotation on controller-manager.
input_row.rationale: RotateKubeletServerCertificate causes the kubelet to both request a serving certificate after bootstrapping its client credentials and rotate the certificate as its existing credentials expire. This automated periodic rotation ensures that the there are no downtimes due to expired certificates and thus addressing availability in the CIA security triad. Note: This recommendation only applies if you let kubelets get their certificates from the API server. In case your kubelet certificates come from an outside authority/tool (e.g. Vault) then you need to take care of rotation yourself. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify that RotateKubeletServerCertificate argument exists and is set to true.
input_row.remediation: Edit the Controller Manager pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- controller-manager.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --feature-gates parameter to include RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true. --feature-gates=RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true Default Value: By default, RotateKubeletServerCertificate is set to "true" this recommendation verifies that it has not been disabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-tls-bootstrapping/#approval-controller 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/267 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/45059 4. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-controller-manager/

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_controller_manager_rotate_kubelet_server_certificate_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-controller-manager Pod specification, typically located in the kube-system namespace as a static Pod manifest (often at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml on the control plane node).
- Use the Kubernetes API or kubectl to retrieve the current configuration:
  - If running as a Pod:  
    ```
    kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-controller-manager -o json
    ```
    - Inspect the `spec.containers[].command` or `spec.containers[].args` fields for the presence of the `--feature-gates` flag.
    - Parse the value of `--feature-gates` and confirm that `RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true` is present and not overridden to false.
- Alternatively, if the controller-manager is not running as a Pod (rare in modern clusters), automation would require access to the process arguments, which is not exposed via the Kubernetes API. In managed Kubernetes environments, this is almost always a Pod.
- **Pass Criteria:** The kube-controller-manager is configured with `--feature-gates=RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true` (either as a standalone flag or within a comma-separated list of feature gates).
- **Fail Criteria:** The `--feature-gates` flag is missing, or `RotateKubeletServerCertificate` is set to `false`, or is not present in the feature gates list (if the default is not true for the cluster version).
- Example command to extract and check:
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-controller-manager -o jsonpath="{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}"
  ```
  or
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-controller-manager -o jsonpath="{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}"
  ```
  Then parse for `--feature-gates` and verify `RotateKubeletServerCertificate=true` is set.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
